title: Pig Island & Koh Tan Snorkeling by Speedboat – Koh Samui Day Trip with Kayaking & Lunch
descriptionHtml:
Discover the famous Pig Island and the tranquil reefs around Koh Tan on a value‑packed speedboat day trip from Koh Samui. Enjoy snorkeling, kayaking, beach time with the island's pigs and a tasty Thai lunch, with convenient hotel pickup included.

Koh Tan (also spelled Ko Tan) and Koh Madsum (Pig Island) are small islands located approximately 4 kilometres off the southern coast of Koh Samui in Surat Thani Province, Thailand. Koh Tan is known for its coral reefs and calm snorkeling waters. Koh Madsum is home to free-roaming pigs on its sandy beach. Both islands are accessible by speedboat or longtail boat from a pier in the south of Koh Samui. GPS coordinates of Koh Tan: 9.4167°N, 100.0333°E. Nearest airport: Koh Samui Airport (USM).

Tour Schedule:
Duration: 5–6 Hours | Departures: Morning & Afternoon Daily
What's Included in Your Tour ▼
  • Round-trip Hotel Transfers from Koh Samui (selected areas)
  • Shared Speedboat Trip to Pig Island & Koh Tan
  • Snorkeling at Reef Spots near Koh Tan (mask & snorkel provided)
  • Beach Time on Pig Island with the Pigs
  • Kayaking (shared equipment)
  • Thai Lunch & Drinking Water
  • Basic Accident Insurance
  • Licensed Local Crew
Not Included ▼
  • Pig Island entrance fee (50 THB/person, payable on the island)
  • Personal shopping and souvenirs
  • Extra drinks or snacks
  • Gratuities for guide and crew
  • Towels (Do not forget to bring your own)
Tour Highlights ▼
  •  Visit Pig Island (Koh Madsum) to relax and see friendly pigs
  •  Snorkel the clear waters and coral reefs around Koh Tan
  •  Enjoy kayaking and plenty of photo opportunities
  •  Economy shared speedboat with hotel transfers & lunch included
  •  Two daily departures for Koh Samui hotel guests
  •  Best value way to see Pig Island & Koh Tan in one day
What to Expect ▼

Morning or afternoon pickup from your Koh Samui hotel and transfer to the pier. After a brief introduction from the crew, enjoy a short speedboat ride across to the islands. Stop at Koh Tan for snorkeling over coral reefs with colorful fish and clear tropical water. Continue to Pig Island (Koh Madsum) for lunch, swimming, relaxing on the beach and meeting the pigs. Free time for kayaking, taking photos and enjoying the sea views before returning to Samui and dropping you back at your hotel. You share the boat with other guests, making this one of the best value ways to see Pig Island and Koh Tan in a single day.

Schedule & Availability ▼

Frequency: Twice daily (morning and afternoon departures)

Operating Days: Daily, subject to weather and sea conditions

Eligibility: Available for hotel guests staying on Koh Samui

Pickup Times: Exact times depend on your hotel location and will be confirmed with your Voucher.

Important Information ▼

Tour Type: Economy shared speedboat tour

Upgrade Available: VIP option with enhanced inclusions available as separate product

What to Bring:

  • Swimwear and towel
  • Reef-safe sunscreen and hat
  • Sunglasses and flip-flops
  • Waterproof bag or phone case
  • Cash for extra drinks or souvenirs

Frequently Asked Questions ▼

Is this speedboat tour to Pig Island suitable for children?
Yes. Children of all ages are welcome. The boat has life jackets for all sizes and the snorkeling areas are calm and shallow. Infants ride free.

Do I need snorkeling experience?
No. The reefs around Koh Tan are shallow and calm, making them ideal for beginners. Snorkeling equipment is provided on board.

Will I actually see pigs on Pig Island?
Yes. Koh Madsum (Pig Island) has free-roaming pigs living on the beach. You walk right up to them on the sand. They are friendly and used to visitors.

What is the Pig Island entrance fee?
There is a small entrance fee of 50 THB per person, paid in cash directly on the island.

Is lunch included on this tour?
Yes. A Thai lunch is included in the tour price and served during the trip.

Where does this tour depart from on Koh Samui?
The tour departs from a pier in the south of Koh Samui. Hotel pickup and drop-off are included from all major areas including Chaweng, Lamai, Bophut, Maenam, and Nathon.

What is the difference between the morning and afternoon departure?
Both departures visit the same islands and include the same activities. The morning trip departs earlier and returns by early afternoon. The afternoon trip departs after lunch and returns by evening.

Cancellation Policy ▼
  • Free cancellation up to 24 hours before departure
  • Changes subject to availability and local operator terms
  • Late cancellations or no-shows may be charged in full
